summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the nocode instrumentation module evaluates JEXL expressions from YAML configuration with unrestricted permissions, enabling arbitrary Java code execution inside the instrumented JVM.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the malicious JEXL expression in the nocode YAML config to be evaluated during method instrumentation, creating the proof file /tmp/evohunt-rce-proof inside the container via java.io.File.createNewFile(), demonstrating arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the splunk-otel-javaagent from source, launched a test Java application with the agent using a malicious nocode YAML config containing a JEXL expression that calls new('java.io.File', '/tmp/evohunt-rce-proof').createNewFile(), and the proof file was created inside the container, confirming the JEXL expression executed arbitrary Java code.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence of the proof file /tmp/evohunt-rce-proof after running the instrumented application. The file is created as a side effect of the JEXL expression evaluation. The script removes any pre-existing proof file before the test to ensure a clean state. The presence of the file after execution confirms that the JEXL expression was evaluated with sufficient permissions to instantiate arbitrary Java classes and call methods on them.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof file /tmp/evohunt-rce-proof was created inside the container as a direct result of JEXL expression evaluation.
The JEXL expression new('java.io.File', '/tmp/evohunt-rce-proof').createNewFile() was loaded from the malicious YAML config and evaluated on a matched method call.
The agent successfully loaded the nocode module, parsed the YAML config, matched the test class, and evaluated the JEXL expression with UNRESTRICTED permissions.
limitations_or_blockers: Building the agent from source requires internet access to download Gradle dependencies, which may take several minutes.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
